title: [InstCombine] disable select folding resulting in extra instructions
summary: The developers generalized the pattern by recognizing that `select` with `icmp` testing a single bit can be transformed into bitwise logic, regardless of the comparison predicate or value types. Originally limited to `and`-mask equality checks, the optimization now handles both `eq`/`ne` predicates and extends to signed comparisons (like `sgt` and `slt`) which implicitly test sign bits. The patch introduces a uniform `ShouldNotVal` flag to invert logic when needed and adds a profitability guard: it avoids creating extra instructions (`and` + `shift` + `xor`) when the bit positions differ between the compared mask and selected constant. This ensures the transformation only fires when it reduces instruction count, maintaining efficiency across scalar, vector, and integer types.
